title: mentha aquatica var. pilosa
intro: Mentha aquatica var. pilosa, also known as American Watermint or Wild Watermint, is a variety of the water mint plant that belongs to the mint family (Lamiaceae) and is native to North America. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties, derived from its essential oils. The health benefits of Mentha aquatica var. pilosa include relief from digestive issues, headaches, and fever, as well as its ability to act as an antispasmodic and a natural breath freshener. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to leverage its medicinal properties and health benefits.
